author: m.kalinin
content: But then you wouldn't pass `get_proposer_head` tests for Gloas, would you? I think it makes sense to backport this changes and either adjust implementation to that or disable a subset of `get_proposer_tests` that are failing due to equivocation counting

author: m.kalinin
content: Also, `get_attestation_score` and `get_weight` have different outcomes, the latter is with proposer boost while the former is not. Given the semantics of `get_proposer_head` it should not sum up the proposer boost and even though it is assumed to be called when proposer boost is vanished, i'd rather use `get_attestation_score` if that is possible to be reflected in implementations

author: m.kalinin
content: I also see that `should_apply_proposer_boost` depends on `is_head_weak` applied to the parent block. And the same concern applies to `should_apply_proposer_boost` -- do we really want the behavior of this function to be dependent on the fork?

author: potuz
content: Backpprting these would break us currently and it would make me at least feel unease with releasing from our main branch. We don't pass the get_proposer_head tests I think anyway as those are optional and I think we never implemented them.

author: m.kalinin
content: I see, then the spec backporting isn't going to break you, you just won't be spec compliant which might be uncomfortable as well tho

author: potuz
content: If the tests pre-gloas don't change we'll be fine

author: m.kalinin
content: but you say you don't run those tests

author: potuz
content: I don't think we run get_proposer_head, can verify when I get out of bed, but it's not clear if these would charge also get_head

author: m.kalinin
content: i guess `get_proposer_head` doesn't affect `get_head`, `should_apply_proposer_boost` which can affect `get_head` has changes related to Gloas and some that doesn't seem to, but it's fine to have it different to Phase0 IMHO
